title: Multimodal upper-limb MI/ME EEG (Jeong et al. 2020)
description: A multimodal EEG dataset comprising motor imagery and motor execution data from 25 healthy subjects performing 11 intuitive upper-limb movement tasks (6 reaching, 3 grasping, 2 wrist twisting) across 3 sessions. The dataset includes 71-channel EEG recordings (60 EEG + 4 EOG + 7 EMG channels) sampled at 1000 Hz, designed for brain-computer interface research and motor control applications.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a 71-channel BrainAmp system (BrainProducts GmbH) with actiCap sensors at 1000 Hz sampling rate. Reference electrode at FCz, ground at Fpz, with online filters (highpass 0.016 Hz, lowpass 1000 Hz). Participants performed 11 motor imagery tasks with visual text cues in a synchronous, offline paradigm across 3 sessions with 550 trials per session (300 reaching, 150 grasping, 100 twisting movements).
